name: PR1 NIS Jans Trail
name_original: Trilho da Barca d'Amieira (PR 11) — percurso circular nos Passadiços de Nisa/Lage da Prata
description: Hike the easy 4.6-mile PR1 NIS Jans Trail in Nisa, featuring river views, unique sculptures, and a ferry crossing.
region_intro: Hiking around Amieira Do Tejo offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Tagus River, its tributaries, and surrounding hills. The region features a mix of riverside paths, agricultural lands, and areas with historical significance. Trails often lead through open countryside and provide views of the local flora and fauna. The terrain generally includes gentle ascents and descents, making it accessible for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Amieira Do Tejo The most popular hiking route is PR1 NIS Jans Trail, an easy 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty. Another top favourite among local hikers is PR4 Conhal Trails, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path that explores varied terrain, including historical "conhal" landscapes. Local hikers also love the Castelo de Amieira – Suspended pedestrian bridge loop from Castelo de Amieira, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through riverside scenery and featuring a notable suspended pedestrian bridge, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Amieira Do Tejo is defined by riverine paths, historical sites, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes to more moderate challenges. The routes in Amieira Do Tejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Amieira Do Tejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Amieira Do Tejo?
A: There are 5 hiking trails documented around Amieira Do Tejo on komoot, offering a variety of experiences from easy riverside strolls to more challenging routes through historical landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: Yes, Amieira Do Tejo offers 3 easy hiking trails. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2244590655/pr1-nis-jans-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR1 NIS Jans Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles (7.4 km) long and provides a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: The terrain around Amieira Do Tejo is diverse, featuring riverside paths along the Tagus River, agricultural lands, and areas with historical significance. Trails often include gentle ascents and descents, making them accessible for various hiking abilities.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Amieira – Suspended pedestrian bridge loop from Castelo de Amieira&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail that takes you through scenic riverside areas and past a notable suspended pedestrian bridge.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting sights. A prominent feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspended pedestrian bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of several routes. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6200332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barca da Amieira Ferry Crossing&lt;/a&gt; and unique cultural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6200293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benbolina Sculpture and Stone Archway&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of riverine paths and historical sites, and the well-maintained trails that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any moderate hiking trails for more experienced hikers?
A: Yes, there are 2 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2242396720/pr4-conhal-trails" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR4 Conhal Trails&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.7-mile (10.9 km) path that explores varied terrain, including historical 'conhal' landscapes, offering a rewarding experience.

Q: Is Amieira Do Tejo suitable for winter hiking?
A: While the region generally experiences mild winters, it's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out. The trails are typically accessible year-round, but some riverside sections might be damp after heavy rain. Always dress in layers and wear appropriate footwear.

Q: Are there any trails that feature the suspended pedestrian bridge?
A: Yes, the suspended pedestrian bridge is a highlight of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passadiços da Barca d'Amieira – Suspended pedestrian bridge loop from Barca da Amieira - Envendos&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience this unique structure, offering a 8.1-mile (13.1 km) moderate hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for hiking in Amieira Do Tejo. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially in the open countryside, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during this season.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options in Amieira Do Tejo?
A: While the main trails are popular, exploring some of the longer, moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ilha do Cabecinho – Pego das Portas de Ródão loop from Santana&lt;/a&gt; might offer a more secluded experience, especially during off-peak hours or weekdays. The region generally provides a peaceful hiking environment.
tips:
This viewpoint is one of those well-kept secrets: it offers an impressive view over the Fratel dam and invites you to take a short break. There is even a small car park for this purpose.

But if you have time, don't stop there. Follow the wooden walkways to Barca da Amieira. It is a short route, but full of surprises, where natural beauty meets art in the middle of the landscape.
It's a brave climb to this place! But once there, the colorful sculptures put a smile on our faces! It's well worth the climb!
Another beautiful swing! But this one is lilac, long and with a beautiful view of the Tagus!
The hike is a highlight in Portugal. There is a lot to experience along the way and you can even ride a rail bike here :)

An extraordinarily beautiful route, with the Tagus always present and lots of entertainment that takes us from Barragem do Fratel to Barca da Amieira, where you can cross the Tagus for free on the boat. It's worth the trip and the hike too!

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the PR1 NIS Jans Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered easy, with minimal elevation changes (around 50 meters up and down). You can expect a pleasant walk suitable for most fitness levels, winding through the scenic Nisa - Lage da Prata area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the PR1 NIS Jans Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, trails in the Nisa - Lage da Prata area, such as the Trilho da Barca d’Amieira, typically have designated parking areas near their starting points. Look for signs for the Passadiços de Nisa or Barca da Amieira, which are key access points for this route.

faqs.question: Is the PR1 NIS Jans Trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas like the Nisa - Lage da Prata Site of Community Importance are dog-friendly. However, always keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Be sure to carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Portugal, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are often ideal, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for hiking. Summers can be hot, so if you hike then, start early in the morning.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Nisa - Lage da Prata area?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Nisa - Lage da Prata Site of Community Importance, there are typically no general entrance fees for accessing the natural area itself. However, specific attractions or facilities along the route might have their own charges. Always check local information before your visit.

faqs.question: What interesting sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable points of interest. You can enjoy panoramic views from the Transparent Tagus Viewpoint - Sky Walk and the Lilac swings. You'll also encounter the unique Benbolina Sculpture and Stone Archway, and the Barca da Amieira Ferry Crossing. Don't miss the Rail Bike at Nisa/Lage da Prata for a different kind of experience.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several local trails. You'll be hiking along parts of the PR1 NIS Trilho das Jans (77% of the route), the Trilho das Jans (77%), and the Trilho da Barca d’Amieira – Passadiços de Nisa (26%). It also passes by the Miradouro Skywalk (8%).

faqs.question: Is the PR1 NIS Jans Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This trail is designed as a circular route, meaning you will end up back near your starting point without having to retrace your steps. This makes for a varied and enjoyable hiking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: As part of the Nisa - Lage da Prata Site of Community Importance, the area is rich in biodiversity. While specific species aren't detailed, you can expect to see various local flora and fauna typical of the Tagus River basin and surrounding natural landscapes. Keep an eye out for birds and small mammals.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities nearby, like cafes or restrooms?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the village of Nisa and the Barca da Amieira area. You can typically find local cafes, restaurants, and basic amenities in these nearby settlements. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy, approximately 7.4 km hike, comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) are essential. Depending on the weather, a light jacket might be useful. Don't forget your camera to capture the scenic viewpoints!

waypoints.name: Rail Bike at Nisa/Lage da Prata
